methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Spinor Energy is a stealth-stage climate & energy company founded by Tyler Nguyen. The broader Climate & Energy sector comprises 21 known peers, of which 1 trades publicly (LNZA) and 8 operate privately. Top private comparables include TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. Despite an available universe of 21 same-sector companies, filtering for stage alignment (stealth), valuation tier similarity, or adjacent-stage development yielded fewer than three datapoints in any narrow cohort. Applying an all-sector median would conflate companies across disparate development phases and capital requirements, introducing substantial bias. No alternative triangulation methods were available from the provided dataset.

This analysis cannot support a specific valuation range at this time. The absence of sufficient same-stage or tier-matched comparables is the binding constraint. To progress, future analysis would require either: (i) expansion of the peer set to include adjacent frontier-energy verticals; (ii) access to transaction multiples or revenue/cost metrics from the identified private peers; or (iii) additional context on Spinor's technical stage, capital intensity, or product timeline relative to the comp universe. The single public comparable (LNZA) operates at a different stage and provides limited direct relevance without additional data harmonization.
